reviews.review_description:
I’ve been playing Vendetta Forever and it’s honestly a lot of funs mooth gameplay and super easy to get into its like super hot and The pacing keeps things engaging, and it’s the kind of game you can jump into for quick sessions but still have a good time.

Not sure why I’m seeing reviews calling the positive feedback fake, because that really doesn’t match my experience at all.

Really great game. Most if not all of the levels are scenes from movies and series.
The movement in the game is a bit annoying, because you can only teleport to those that you killed. The jokes on the levels are great, the design ia great, the graphics reminds super hot, the core fun of thw game is great.

This is my first review and I have played  quite a few games that I like. But this is the first game I feel compelled to review.
This game is the best quest game I’ve played so far.   played so far. It’s so cool , fun and innovative. I thought Super Hot was a great game until I played this.

To prove I’m not a bot Pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis Pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis Pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis Pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis okay anyway this game is very fun I got I would say 10ish hours of play time and it’s a great game worth the price don’t listen to those 50 yr old men who don’t want to commit mass murder would give 4.5 stars because not enough scenes

This is the type of game where you'll keep replaying the same level over and over until you know where every enemy is and can beat the level no as quickly and efficiently as possible.


Edit: just read some of the negative reviews. Those guys are losers who turned off the game after 20 minutes because they're not good enough. Look at better reviews like eznobn's

The game is visually like superhot (classic game) but the movement and time dilation mechanisms are just not fun by comparison.  Given that is essentially the entire game it is just not worth your time even on Horizon+.  Graphics are not polished and the gameplay loop is very poor.  Very likely paid reviews are manipulating ratings.

This game has a great concept although the movement could use a fix and the difficulty ramps up way too fast, I typically play a huge catalog of high action games but this just annoyed me so much with the instant chaos and not much time to get a grip for the controls. It feels like there is too much going on and could definitely be better.

reviews.review_title: Trial and Error : they expect perfection
reviews.review_description: Got an S Rating on the 1st stage the 2nd stage could not get more then a B rating with 11 kills and 0 damage (cleared the area) I don't like when games want you to play their way 100% thier perfect way.   You randomize the weapons enemies drop but script when the enmies appear around the corner only when you turn your head. So if the weapons are random drops, how do you score what an S rating is?  You put armor on the enemy and  the aim is not working all the time,  is it aim assist or not?  If not why you trying to stylize it, at least Pistol Whip understood the assignment.

Closest game I've played to a John wick experience. Music is great, it distinguishes itself beautifully from games like superhot despite the art style being low poly and similar, it has its own identity. 10/10 would subscription again

reviews.review_title: Another hit from the masters of action
reviews.review_description:
Comparing this game to SUPERHOT is fundamentally wrong. In SUPERHOT, you’re cool because you cheat with time. Here, you’re cool because you actually are — because you managed to read the situation and react fast enough. At the same time, this isn’t Action Hero, where every level has to be memorized. Everything here is far more dynamic and honest: if you can read the situation and shoot accurately, you win — even on your first attempt.

In that sense, this game is much closer to the first Hotline Miami: you act on instinct, and only afterward realize that you somehow survived.

Highly recommended to anyone who loves reactive action games set to great music.

I found the controls very patchy and not very reliable. Graphically it's superhot meets pistol whip, but it's not as good as either of those games. 

I found it got very hard too quickly which put me off. Played for 20 minutes and decided it's not for me.

Oh my god! This is really good fun! I love it. You definitely feel like John Wick, I've just completed it but will be back, time and time again. You can play for 10 minutes or so and be exhausted! Replay value is great, getting your kills and time down to an art!
I love how smooth the throwing weapons feel and how good it feels to pass every level, the addition of the “super hot” movement modifier really just makes it feel like super hot Pt2
I'm convinced the people who give this game 5 stars either didn't play the game or have the brain power of a pitbull
This has been a blast. It really scratches that SUPERHOT itch, but differentiates itself from SUPERHOT. It is also a great workout if you keep trying to beat your time.

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR shooting games
reviews.review_description: Man, once you start getting the hang of things, you end up feeling like john wick. Super smooth gameplay, nice graphics, and FUN!

This game really deserves more attention then it got. It's very, very good. I appreciate the challenge and the grading system at the end of each stage adds some replayability. Highly recommended for fans of shooters and games requiring quick thinking and accuracy.

Such and awesome game, with a ton of levels and create music. This guy put a lot into this game. If you like Super Hot, I know you’ll love this. Hope this guy continues to create. Wanted to show some love and drop a review. Definitely get this one, you won’t regret it.

I just got this game not too long ago and already love playing the hell out of it. It's so much better than SUPERHOT, and more interactive than PISTOL WHIP because you're not just standing in the middle of the screen. This is recommended for those who like moving at a fast & efficient pace.

reviews.review_title: An experience only possible in VR
reviews.review_description: This is actually in my top 3 all time "VR action" games. There's just certain games which are not achievable elsewhere. This is pure fun and makes you feel like an action star. Great concept from the team and fantastic execution.

reviews.review_title: So fun and can be accidental exercise
reviews.review_description: I use this game for cardio. You can keep it chill or crank up the intensity to nail the perfect flow and beat the clock. It's feels like the next level of Pistol Whip but without the killer music. There are tons of weapon choices, mechanics, tips, and tricks to perfect. I've played for the better part of a year and haven't even really got into the modifiers yet. Nothing is as satisfying as "scene perfected".

Good game but the graphics are off from it’s time 
Yeah, overall good ￼￼

From the very first frames, I fell in love with the game's minimalist art design, which at first glance seems to be the love child of two previous VR hits: Pistol Whip's dad and Superhot's mom couldn't deny the act, even if the kid moves! Vendetta Forever comes from that segment of (VR) games where there is no story, no cutscenes, and no dialogue, and it doesn't evoke any thoughts or feelings in us, but we don't feel their absence for a single moment as we are dizzy with the raw, powerful flow experience of the gunplay. The system, called "Lo-Kill-Motion" and learnt in a matter of seconds, is a rail shooter game mechanic operating with slow-motion elements, which, as a combination of 6DoF controllers and a 360° playing field, can only be interpreted in virtual reality. We cannot freely change our position, but by eliminating our opponents and jumping into their place, we take over their current weapons and thus progress through the relatively quick levels from enemy to enemy. The logical puzzles hidden in the high-action heroics complete the challenge, with the optimal order of elimination and fast execution being equally important on longer levels. The creator spices up the new levels with numerous small innovations, one of the most memorable moments of the game is racing through a location shrouded in complete darkness, guided only by the light of the muzzle flashes. Inspired by its predecessors but capable of displaying its own individuality, this action-packed game is a prime example of how a good idea based on the feeling of presence in virtual reality can be turned into an entertaining and pleasantly challenging fast-paced action game with simple gameplay using just a few buttons. I never felt that the stylized visuals were lacking, and the clean gameplay is particularly good at not getting lost in the details of the visuals, but rather letting us lose ourselves in the feeling of "being there."

reviews.review_title: The best Quest game I’ve played yet
reviews.review_description: The game looks very good, the controls are easy enough, I was never into superhot. This game is great though!

Similar to super hot and action hero. It's a good game but if you make a mistake like drop your weapon, or if your weapon is out if amo, you have to start over. Teleport back to previous spot what's the point you can't defend yourself with no weapons and that's a downer but not sure if it's the point of the game tbh.
For me the better Version of Superhot.

reviews.review_title: Must have if you like shooting
reviews.review_description:
This game is great for short adrenaline bursts - from the moment you open the app it puts you straight in to teach you the controls - grab knife - stub this guy- he's dropped a gun grab it - shooting this guy- blue gun - grab it to teleport - shoot another guy - done here's the menu screen. 

You don't have to unlock levels you get access to all of them - if its too hard (it was for me) you can lower the difficulty and add modifiers

You die...you die a lot...but you restart and its instant...and the levels are short and you're learning each time where the enemies are and in what order they'll shoot. 

Even if you have pistol whip and a bunch of first person shooters this game is its own thing and really fun. My only feedback is I wish when you died there was instant feedback (like red skull 💀)instead of a 1 second delay where time slows down before showing the ui

reviews.review_title: Yeah, I bought a Quest to play this game
reviews.review_description:
The more I play Vendetta Forever, the more it cements itself as one of my all time top games 💯 You know when something is made so well, that you enjoy it even though it has things you don’t usually like? THAT’s creativity and quality. I dislike teleporting in VR, and have little interest in replaying levels or leaderboards. Yet Vendetta Forever’s movement style is so well thought out within brilliantly crafted diverse levels, wrapped up in a highly entertaining package that makes me regularly genuinely smile and don’t want to stop playing. I bought a Quest primarily to play this exact game wirelessly lol.

There’s so much to love. The game is simply endless, creative, awesome FUN. It is NOT “Superhot 2” as some say – it’s far beyond that and is completely its own thing with substantially more content, imagination and quality. This is a ‘your fav character’ simulator heavily covered in lots of unique icing. All those spy, action, revenge and adventure movies/games you loved growing up and in recent times, you can now be the star of. From Wick and Bond, to Lara and Indiana, and even some Chan, Stallone and piratey Sparrow vibes. I love story-driven games, but as this game’s marketing says: this is cutting to the good bits 🔪

The icing is it’s clearly crafted by someone who loves a range of classics, so each level is completely different with its own style, quirks and ideas. Examples:
- Blind – genius. Using your hearing in complete darkness, only seeing glimpses of light from noises, throwing objects to distract enemies and identify their positions. One of the most exhilarating, coolest VR experiences I've had in a decade of VR that I return to.
- Vertigo – another awesome level, falling through a roof window while shooting around you at enemies on a staircase like Agent 47 or one of the MacManus saints.
- You can even experience the Wanted scene, standing on a train and swinging your arm to curve a bullet into a building to get the target 🔫
- Planes, prison breaks, samurais, snipers, nightclubs, supermarket shootouts, cowboys, speakeasies, battlefield trenches, submarines, even space with laser guns.

Simple enough to feel like an agent of chaos, yet lots to master. Dip in for 10 mins, or spend hours refining your strategy and skill. Very rewarding. I was blasting through levels, then really liked a stealthy one. 3 hours later, replaying it continuously, I got the highest score in the world. Felt awesome. It’s also a great regular workout if you want it to be. It’s part of my exercise routine, tho I am literally rolling around and getting proper into it 😂

Lastly, the collectibles/unlockables are cool and not annoying. Mutators keep it fresh, different and challenging (yes there’s a ‘time moves when you do’ mutator!). The music and sound work is brilliant. The slo-mo when grabbing is not only cool but becomes strategic. The game is a goldmine of witty punchlines. bHaptics support is amazing – TactSuit and TactSleeve feel great, even attention to detail during the game’s intro. Physical gunstock support is coming. And the Deleted Scenes DLC, which is FREE, steps it up a notch further with more surprises – my fav is the space level.

The future? I’d love to see more experimental visual styles, moods, genres, music and characters (the new DLC is a great example), more action set pieces, vehicles (car chases, sci-fi starfighters), maybe some horror, a dinosaur getaway level, and a ‘random continuous mode’. Essentially ramping up the wilder side even further!

I know, a crazy long review, but this game deserves it. The developer, Zander (yes, 1 guy) deserves it. Buy it. Enjoy it. THANK YOU Zander for making such an amazing fun game. It makes me nod to my kid-self, getting to embody all those characters and experience these scenes as an adult. The sheer joy it brings me is worth many more times the asking price. You deserve tons of praise, resources and support.

BUY IT!!!!!!!!!

reviews.review_title: Great when it works, some missions buggy
reviews.review_description: There's a ton of missions, and most levels work well. Don't be discouraged when you repeatedly finish a mission and it won't move you to the final area (sky diving keeps floating above ground after defeating everyone even when I grab the item, saw someone finish on YouTube doing the same steps, so it apparently works for some players some of the time), or a tape next to you can't be picked up or shot, etc. Gotta let that go and skip to the other levels here, they aren't order gated. Also I wish there was a way to turn off 180 turns, which I never mean to do but frequently happens unintentionally when I'm just trying to turn, incredibly frustrating. Otherwise, it's great, and I'd still recommend it.
Thumbs way up!!!!  Really fun time in VR!!

reviews.review_title: One of my favorite VR games
reviews.review_description: When I bought this game I liked it a lot but played casually testing all tracks. But that was for me personally wrong way to play this game. These kind of games are best, when you grind the same track over and over to get the higher and higher score. My time flyes and I get a good sweat. And there's more than enought ways to challenge yourself. I have used and I will use many hours to this game

1 Star reviewers hating on the game because they stink at it is hilarious
I've played 60 hours and I don't plan on stopping anytime soon

It's like the lovechild of Pistol Whip with its high octane immediacy and crisp, high abstraction visuals (which definitely add to the vibe rather than detract) and the game you wished Super Hot was... (which is still super hot, that games fire but this is just 👌).

The sheer wealth of levels is remarkable, each one superbly crafted to a specific theme (many evoking famous moments from action movies - that Equilibrium gun kata door breach is siiick) Much variety between the gameplay in each level - one even purely relies on noise and gun flashes to find the enemies.

Similarities between Pistol whip end with the music not being tied to the gunplay but the soundtrack is equally pumping and slaps during the action.

Most surprising though is on paper I wouldn't have liked the idea of a game that purely relies on teleportation locomotion. I love freedom and being able to move where I want. It's to the games credit it makes that style of movement fit the gameplay so darn well.

Factor in the heaps of modifiers to adjust gameplay (even a slow time a la super hot) coupled with difficulty levels that all combine for end score totals and hidden collectibles and you're looking at a very sturdy amount of content plus 60 odd leaderboards to chase.

Really unlikely you'll play this game and *not* feel like a John Wick Assassin. Buy with confidence ✌️

I'm still getting mysteriously stuck in some missions. It must be a bug. Despite that, this game is so much fun on hard setting and with aim assist turned off. Even the shooting gallery is good enough on its own. You won't be disappointed if you purchase this game.

I have been playing computer games since the original Doom - this is easily the coolest game I've ever played. There are things I'd spend far more time doing, like sim racing, but for 20 mins of action, this is incredible.

Honestly, one of my favorite games for the Oculus. It has like every amazing fight scene from every movie ever made duplicated in this game. I also love the modifiers you can add onto levels to make it exactly like Super Shot VR where time only moves when you do. It is fast-paced and highly addictive!!! I highly recommend if you like shooters and games back in the day like Golden Eye 007, where you need to beat a time to unlock cheat codes or in this case some modifiers by doing various challenges.

👏🏾                       👏🏾                            👏🏾                           👏🏾            👏🏾           👏🏾                       👏🏾                            👏🏾                      👏🏾   👏🏾             👏🏾        👏🏾         👏🏾         👏🏾        👏🏾      👏🏾    👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾 👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾  👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾👏🏾 At first sight, "It's just Super Hot and Whiplash." As though that could be wrong. It's a beautiful baby. Hands down the most fun I've had doing cardio for 45 mins. Well done.
Really one of the best VR experiences I’ve ever had every mission has some type of gimmick to it making them all feel different. Plus with the modifiers the replay of the missions make it even better

The gameplay is out of this world. Combat is fluid, they implemented the teleportation mechanic really well and it feels natural. There's tons of replay ability, with there being over 50 different maps, exploring unique ideas with multiple different routes in certain maps. Mutators to spice up your game, making it easier or challenging. Tons of achievements as well as collectibles. The music is the thing that puts it all together, amplifying the experience and making it genuinely feel like you are a part of a movie. This game deserves more recognition, the devs put a lot of time and effort creating this masterpiece and it is definitely worth the money.
I was looking for something to add to the mix after pistol whip. This scratches the itch, and is a blast!

reviews.review_title: This is the John Wick game you're after.
reviews.review_description:
I certainly didn't expect a VR puzzle shooter to be one of my 2024 games of the year but here we are.

The game hinges on a premise not dissimilar to Superhot, in that you're choreographing an action scene in a minimalist polygonal setting, except time doesn't stop here to let you consider your next move. No, where the fun of Superhot is more simply solving its time-stop scenarios, Vendetta's choreography asks a bit more, and hinges on the discovery of that elusive perfect run. Picking apart a scene and then acting it out in real time over as many runs as it takes to get it right, chasing scores through perfect accuracy to rival Bond and making sure to nail your Neo-like bullet avoidance.

While your main input is more often than not shooting, this is a clever action game at its core that emphasises replaying levels over and over to learn enemy placements and patterns, work out your ideal movements through a level (reaching for blue weapons doubles as bullet time and teleportation, lending the game and on-rails feel but often allowing experimentation of your specific path), and hopefully not get shot in the process. But the John Wick of it all comes from an element of adapting to the weapon you have, throwing guns when the mood takes you and even resorting to the odd pencil. And that's all alongside finding little ways within levels to be clever, from optional traps that would have Indiana Jones on his toes to a sneakily hidden weapon that suddenly opens up a fresh strategy.

And the levels would be satisfying and moreish with the basics of the gameplay design alone, but there's a reason I've name dropped a few notable names along the way - Vendetta loves action films. Many of the levels (and there is a hugely generous spread that range from shootouts and stealth operations to duels and pirate skirmishes) pull from a gamut of classic movies to offer action hero power trips that will have you grinning from ear to ear, even on your 25th run.

If you like Superhot or Pistol Whip, this is a no brainer. If you're looking for an action game that builds its thrills on foundations of smart thinking, dive in. If you're looking for a VR game with hours of satisfying arcadey shoot-bang gameplay, this is an extremely safe bet. Basically if you're even vaguely interested, get it.

developer_response.body:
Hi Dalagonash,

We appreciate you for leaving such a detailed and lovely review! and yes you're correct we do love our action films :D

We're glad to hear that you are experiencing a bunch of variety from each level by understanding enemy placements, patterns and ideal weapons to use. You also have your modifiers that make the levels play out completely differently.

Thank you again for the positive feedback and we hope to see you continue to enjoy Vendetta Forever not only on your 25th run, but on your 50th and 100th run <3

reviews.review_description: I bought this just because I've either beaten or got as far as i could in all my games and bought this as a whatever it'll be a quick time waster thinking it was a super hot clone. I was wrong. This game is an absolute blast and Im not even half way done. Its very fast and intense and i cant wait to play more of it.

If John Wick, Max Payne, and Superhot had a VR baby, it would be Vendetta Forever. This game is a full-throttle, high-flying, gun-fu masterpiece that makes you feel like an action hero from the moment you pull the trigger.

The movement system is genius—every kill propels you forward, turning every fight into a deadly dance of bullets, slow-motion takedowns, and mid-air weapon swaps. It’s easy to pick up but has a crazy skill ceiling, rewarding precision, creativity, and pure style.

The level design is straight out of a cult action flick, from chaotic saloon shootouts to high-stakes airplane hijackings. Every scenario makes you want to replay it just to pull off the perfect sequence. And the soundtrack? Absolute fire—it fuels the adrenaline rush.

If you love fast-paced, tactical VR shooters that make you look and feel cool, this is a must-buy. Vendetta Forever isn’t just a game—it’s an action-movie simulator, and you’re the star.

Vendetta Forever, of course, is the final version of Bang Bang Slice. The game involves making your way through FPS puzzle situations by teleporting to the location of the last baddie that you shot. You need to be reactive and decisive, and there is a decent amount of exercise involved in the form of dodging bullets. Gameplay is very polished and the graphics are simple yet highly stylized. Replay value is almost limitless as I find myself wanting to repeatedly improve my grade for each level. In the vein of Super Hot and other puzzle shooters, this is so much more than a button-masher  and a must-have for your library.

l i n k t r . e e / q u a c k b i r t h

reviews.review_title: Shoot bad guys - in the face.
reviews.review_description:
Wait, maybe I'm the bad guy? 

This game is pretty awesome with addictive gameplay keeping me coming back for more.

The learning curve was initially very steep for me. After a bit of practice though (pirate party is really good for this, because all the guns are 1-shot so it forces you to get into the shoot-drop-grab cycle) it becomes second nature. I initially hated it when I got a knife/throwing-star instead of a gun, but now it's so cool. Getting your first triple kill with a throwing weapon makes you feel like a boss..

Replayability is very good. There are mutators that make the game harder and up your score. This then allows you to get higher on the leader boards.

I love this game so much. It’s fast moving and has so much variety-there is always something new. And it’s so physically involving that it doubles as a workout! I especially love the bloodless mode (Gayle’s Birthday version). One of the best games I’ve ever played!
I can't put this thing down. I just keep going and then all of a sudden I'm drippin it sweat because I'm doing 360s throwing knives and getting head shots. Get it. You won't regret it.

Despite the fast action, this is not a casual game or a sandbox. Do the tutorials (including advanced). Adjust your settings and learn the buttons. Treat the levels like a puzzle to be solved and not whack-a-mole. The easiest guy to shoot first is often a trap.

When its working well you feel superhuman and clever, and the developer is actively updating and interacting with the community on improvements.

reviews.review_title: The actioned packed game you NEED
reviews.review_description: Get it. There is something about this game that is very refreshing, and that something is the fluidity, gunslinging, and quick thinking you experience in the 10 secs to 2 mins levels. The time you spend on a level might seem short, but you will find yourself replaying them multiple times until you feel like the action hero this game needs you to be. ￼It’s a great game to throw your friends into and watch them be impressed. Do it. It’s worth it.

This game is just excellent. Feels great to play, with a fantastic soundtrack and solid game mechanics. It's very reminiscent of something like Superhot, but where that game feels like it wants you to go as slow as possible, this actually challenges you to go as fast as you can. When it's firing on all cylinders, it feels like you're Neo, dodging bullets and just leaping through the environment. Fun to just run through the levels, and excellent if you like going for high-scores.

It’s really a great game.. ACTUALLY NO it’s MORE than that this is the best game I’ve ever played it’s so fun and addicting you won’t stop ontil your done with them all and there is A-LOT so you can get great play time, the few last levels like the colasium is great I can always come back to it so BUY IT! 
thank you for reading 😁
A

And Developers. Thank 
You for this game and I’m also I think it would be a good idea for people to make custom levels to ensure infint variety of levels and never ending fun
An absolute blast. I was hesitant at first because of the graphics but this is the most fun ive had aside from Batman or beat Saber

So like many will say it like superhot and pistal whip but it never quite matchs there comfort levels, some levels work better than others, but you can get quite disoritated flying around location and require fast movement to turn your body and shoot. You need fairly good vr legs. So i would recommend this if your a fan of the two games just be pepared for more movement than the other games.

No doubt, this is a 5-star game. It’s impressive how “simple” ideas can become legendary when time and passion deliver quality like this. I played the demo for hours, and it quickly became one of my favorite games in my entire Oculus library. At first, I hesitated when I saw the $35 CAD price tag, but the demo proved to be a gateway to success, and the final product is like “Superhot” and “Pistol Whip” blended together and polished to perfection.

I usually find it hard to give “perfect” scores to games that restrict movement this much—it’s not 2016 anymore—but the care and attention behind this game make it clear the developers had a vision. It’s similar to how I learned to adapt to teleportation in The Light Brigade. When the gameplay is designed with such care, it’s worth respecting, and this game absolutely delivers.

Go buy it, go play it, sweat a lot, and then start over. And while you’re at it, don’t miss The Light Brigade—a criminally underrated gem.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this lovely review! It means so much to get this kind of feedback!

reviews.review_description: It’s very fun. Quick to get a hang of. They make it so restarting levels is fast. So quick that I felt myself messing up one section and just restarting to go faster. That quick restart ability makes this incredibly fun and addictive. Also, holy cow I played for like an hour and barely put a dent in the levels?! I had sweat dripping down my face in such a good way. A+

Don’t sleep on Vendetta Forever, this game’s got 50 very fun creative scenarios that can all be played a variety of ways.  Whether it’s the course you take, the modifiers you’ve got on, or the level of difficulty you’ve selected, I guarantee you’ll be playing through all of these scenes multiple times trying to better your score and unlock new weapons and modifiers.  All that and a killer (pun intended) soundtrack to boot!  Highly recommended!

reviews.review_title: Best VR game I've played in ages
reviews.review_description: I started by playing the demo and was blown away. This game is crazy good and a must grab if you liked other hot titles like superhot. This game is hours of fun on end and very competitive to be the fastest to complete a scene or get the highest score. If I could give more than 5 stars I would!

developer_response.body:
Hi Lemony,

Thank you for the review! We're very happy to hear that the demo did a great job convincing you to play the full game :D

Looking forward to seeing you on the leaderboards!

- Richi

reviews.review_description: Wow so much fun...

A really fun gameplay loop that feels like an offshoot of Superhot. The levels, taking set pieces from action movies, can be incredibly satisfying when you find ‘the flow’. However, not all levels are created equal and I think there will be 3-4 that people find frustrating or too long, I certainly did. More weapon mechanics become available as you progress and there are mutators to unlock as well as different levels of difficulty. Hoping to see even more content for this in the future.

developer_response.body: Hey! Thank you so much for your feedback. We're sorry to hear that some levels felt a bit frustrating for you, but we hope you loved the rest of the game! We'll pass on your comments to the team. - Sean

reviews.review_title: I Think I'll Get a Lot of Time Out It
reviews.review_description:
I played the demo for Vendetta Forever and liked it, but didn't love it. I read online this has 60 levels, and one of my big concerns was 'oh man this will take me like 2 hours to beat and I'll never touch it again', and the price is high for something like that. I kept seeing really good press for the game though and figured I'd give it another shot. 

I think the big difference in my demo time vs my time with it tonight is putting it on the hardest difficulty which makes every good shot or throw so much more rewarding due to the reduced aim assist (I think there might be a very small bit still there). The game is so much more difficult that I spent about a half hour just doing the first two levels, the first of which I got a good leaderboard rank on and the second I'm not satisfied with and will go back to a couple more times before moving on to level 3.

Playing on the hardest difficulty is a brutal workout. Not quite Thrill of the Fight levels of exhausting but I'm beat up in a different way than just winded. I kept dropping to my knees on my vinyl floors to dodge bullets. Finally I wised up and put down some yoga mats side by side so I could slide, jump back on my butt or back Max Payne style, and I've never had a game make me feel quite like this, like a real action hero, and it's making me work for it. 

My biggest fear of this being a short experience has been assuaged. I'll be playing this for a long time, especially if the difficulty ramps up at all over successive levels. To spend 30 minutes on just the first two levels, knowing I've probably got another 15 minutes to go to really nail that second level, then considering I have, what, 58 more levels to go? The cost is more than justified, and I can't put a price on something that has me knee sliding around my living room and the smile that comes with it.  This is clearly a very early review and if I run into any issues I'll update, but very excited about this one.

developer_response.body:
Hi brianc515,

So happy to hear that you're enjoying the game on the highest difficulty! We try to provide a variety of experience for players to enjoy in different ways.

Replayability is an important factor for us and we're glad you're enjoying your time perfecting each level :D

- Richi

reviews.review_description: It's a great mix of Superhot and Pistol Whip

Edit 2:

The Dev’s reply was true, they added an option to disable turning with joystick. Now that I’ve been able to play without issue, the game is amazing.

It is reminiscent of SUPERHOT and Pistol Whip with their features and this is one of the few games where I feel like I am in a John Wick movie. 

Super excited to see the future updates. 


————————
Edit 1:
I just saw the devs’ reply, super excited to play! Will update later.

Original Post:
As soon as I saw the trailer on YouTube i immediately purchased it, but not even a minute into gameplay and my stick drift took control. It's extremely difficult for me to play and I wish there was a way to disable turning with joystick.

So please developers, if you're reading this, please add an option to remove turning with joystick.

reviews.review_title: Dev team listens and game is amazing
reviews.review_description: I really really really enjoy this game. The feeling of performing slick moves straight from an action movie (whilst the assist makes them easy) feels unrivaled. I have enjoyed every stage. My one complaint with the game was the lack of random stages, you have to pick your stage or go through them in a set order. But upon mentioning this is in the discord I got an actual response saying "we'll look into it" which is amazing to get a direct response from the devs.
This is a great game buy this now

For anyone who was lucky enough to nab the original free version aka bang bang slice slice, Vendetta forever adds enough incentives and updates to make what may seem like a steep price for a game you've already played worth it. For those of you who have never heard of bang bang slice slice do yourself a favour right now and buy this game. And you are also lucky, because you get to experience this awesome game for the very first time in the form it was always meant to be in :)
This is the best VR game when it comes to pure gameplay. Levels take less than a minute to complete and are endlessly replay-able. I don’t see myself uninstalling this game ever.
That's Bang Bang Slice (which I already really liked), just more polished and refined. Get it!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We're glad you enjoy the great work of MeatSpace :D

reviews.review_description: One of the Best vr entry games on meta store right now.

Incredibly fun, shooting and throwing weapons never felt so good!  Teleport to the falling weapon of an enemy you just dropped, that's how you move and it works! Short levels that can feel like puzzles in terms of who you kill and how, and plenty of levels! Spiritual successor to superhot and brilliant in its own way.  Give it a try, I love this game!
cool game, i love how unlike most games it actually has aim assist to make u feel like john wick even tho u dont have the best aim

This game is just fun, with plenty of content to boot. The movement system is novel and perfect for VR, in one level it makes you feel like you are jumping over store counters, in the next like you are running on top of a train or falling down a plane, all with the same easy way to get around. To be honest though I would knock 1/2 a star due to some levels being near impossible (Terminal Velocity and Vertigo comes to mind), but it's not enough to lose a full star being that the rest of the game is so outstanding.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! It is so good to hear you enjoyed the variety of action experiences from each level :D

reviews.review_description:
JUST PLAYED FOR THE FIRST TIME TODAY . BRILLIANT … , ABSOLUTELY BRILL !!! 
 JUST LIKE OTHER’S HAVE SAID IT’S “ JUST LIKE THE JOHN WICK MOVIE’S ! “ . 
 A FRENZY FOR FIRING FAN’S !!
LARGE ASSORTMENT OF ALL KINDS OF WEAPON’S  ALL WITH PINPOINT ACCURACY .ON TARGET , WELL DONE !
MY NEW FAVORITE GAME !!!
Fun fun game.  It makes u feel like John Wick in the flesh.  Just purchased a few days ago and can't wait to jump in to the game for a few mins daily.  Great job.

reviews.review_title: Best VR coffee table game yet!!
reviews.review_description:
What an awesome ride! This game hits a magical sweet-spot for me where I can cue it up for a quick play-sesh and have a great time without getting wrapped up in some long story or campaign. It’s also been great for showing off my headset to friends! Graphics and performance are top notch on my Quest 2. 

I live in a tiny apartment and usually play with a stationary boundary from my office chair so I don’t crash into things, and the lo-kill-motion mechanic turned out to be a huge boon. It makes me feel like I’m dashing around without actually needing a bigger space.

Highly recommend doing the advanced tutorial and playing around in the practice range if you get stuck—those saved me a bunch of headache when I was getting my bearings early on!

Respect to the solo dev, incredible that such a fun game came from one guy. Definitely pick this one up or try the demo if you’re on the fence! 10/10 from me!

Love this game has so many unique mechanics like teleportation when you pick up a item and I love how it’s kinda in the style of super hot and it also has a ton of levels so it’s pretty fun my favorite being mile high club 5/5 would recommend 👍

This might be my next gaming obsession. So much fun to play trying to perfect the run. 

One thing that slows me down a bit, is trying to distinguish if I have a throwing weapon or a gun waiting for me. Is there a way to color code them so I can tell the difference easier/faster?

I am super impressed with the game. Need to charge my quest and rest up a bit to get ready for the next session.
This game reminds me of Pistol Whip in the way it makes you feel like John Wick. The teleportation feels very natural and assassin-like. A 10/10 game that achieves what Superhot aspires to.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the great review!

reviews.review_description:
This is the first time I’ve ever felt inclined to leave a review for anything at all. I was looking for games on YouTube and this popped up as one of the recommended games to play. I figured well maybe I’ll give it a try and if I don’t like it, I’ll refund it within the 2 hour limitation like I feel happens more than not with vr games I’ve tried so far. 

BUT THIS GAME was INCREDIBLY fun straight off the jump. I feel like John Wick if he was slightly overweight and in the MATRIX.

I got to this one area where you stab a guy with a freaking pencil ✏️ and it was invigorating. 

This game is very SIMPLE to pick up and learn, I even got my girlfriend to give it a try and she stole my headset from me for a while because she couldn’t put the game down. & she’s never played a VR game in her life. Although, as she and I both learned even though it’s simple to get the hang of it is not an easy game to master. I only have one “S” rating on a level so far and I plan to go back and turn my A ratings into S ratings very soon. 

100% recommend this game to anybody that likes to shoot things and almost every level has some sort of replayability from choosing who to attack first or what path you want to take in order to reach the exit the fastest. It’s 2am and I lost track of time so I can’t wait to play more tomorrow after work. 

11/10 game would be neat if there was some sort of 1v1 duel mode with the slow down mechanics and weapon teleporting in the future. I can’t wait to see what updates are going to come soon. 

Thank you devs.

developer_response.body:
Hey there,

We're so glad you're enjoying Vendetta Forever so much! The fact that the game made you feel inclined to leave a review means so much to us :D

It is very good to hear that you want to replay every level and achieve the highest score. Thank you again for playing and for getting others to try it too!

reviews.review_title: This is it. This is the game.
reviews.review_description: This is the perfect blend of immersive/active FPS. This is the stuff of dreams. Very very very well done. Game of the year material. I’d pay double or triple for this.
